source: Verified specifications from SG Standard with Vintage Pickguard. Neck thickness 20.32mm/22.225mm, joint angle 4 deg, headstock angle 17 deg, body join at 19th fret confirmed from spec sheet 2026-03-29. DXF body outline available: DXF-00-Gibson-SG.dxf.
notes: The SG (Solid Guitar) replaced the Les Paul in 1961 and became Gibson's best-selling electric. Double cutaway allows full access to upper frets - body joins at 19th fret, documented in spec. The SG body is thinner and lighter than the LP.

standard.description: Gibson SG Standard - double cutaway solid mahogany, set neck at 19th fret, dual humbuckers, tune-o-matic bridge. The definitive SG configuration.

neck.depth_note: Neck-only depth (back of neck to underside of fretboard). Add fretboard thickness (~5mm) for total stack.
neck.body_join_note: Documented in spec: set neck joint at 19th fret. Upper fret access is excellent due to double cutaway.

headstock.note: Standard Gibson open-book headstock. Slightly thicker than ES-335 (15.08mm vs 14.6mm).

body.weight_note: SG is significantly lighter than LP - thinner body, no maple cap. Typical weight 2.7-3.2 kg.

eds_1275.description: Gibson EDS-1275 Double Neck - SG body extended to accommodate two full necks. Lower: 6-string SG (628.65mm). Upper: 12-string SG (628.65mm). Body join fret and neck specs identical to SG Standard on both necks.

sg_junior.description: Gibson SG Junior 1963 - single P-90 pickup, wrap-around bridge, dot inlays. Simplified hardware, same body/neck geometry.

sg_custom.description: Gibson SG Custom - multi-ply binding, three pickups, gold hardware, block inlays.

comparison_to_explorer.body_join_fret: SG joins at documented 19th fret vs Explorer TBD 19-20
comparison_to_explorer.body_construction: both solid mahogany - SG thinner and lighter
